analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 9 finding(s) from 10 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: JTAG authentication is completely missing. The design loads jtag_key but never uses it for access control.
findings.vulnerability_category: initiate DMI transactions / Debug Module internal state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external JTAG controller Operation: initiate DMI transactions Object: Debug Module internal state Expected guard: jtag_key comparison gating dmi_req_valid Observed behavior: No jtag_key comparison logic exists; the key is loaded into a pass register but never compared. DMI requests proceed unconditionally. Path: JTAG pins -> dmi_jtag_tap -> dmi_jtag (dmi_req_valid_o) -> dm_csrs Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Unauthenticated JTAG access to entire debug module, including halt, CSR access, and memory reads.

findings.summary: SBA can read and write ROM2 without any access control.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write ROM2 via SBA / ROM2 module contents / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug module SBA Operation: read/write ROM2 via SBA Object: ROM2 module contents Expected guard: debug SBA authorization or address filter to block ROM2 accesses Observed behavior: No address filter exists; ROM2 is writable and readable. SBA can set sbaddress to ROM2 base (0x0021_0000) and perform read/write. Path: dm_top -> i_dm_sba -> AXI master -> crossbar -> ariane_peripherals (i_rom2) Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Attacker can extract JTAG key, AES key, or modify them, breaking system security.

findings.summary: Debug mode elevates privilege to M and does not restrict CSR writes, violating expected protection.
findings.vulnerability_category: write machine-mode CSRs / CSR registers (mstatus, mepc, etc.) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug module (via abstract commands or DMI) Operation: write machine-mode CSRs Object: CSR registers (mstatus, mepc, etc.) Expected guard: debug mode should not allow writes to protected CSRs; privilege checks should still apply. Observed behavior: When debug_mode_q is high, priv_lvl_o is forced to M, allowing writes to all M-mode CSRs. No lock-bit or debug-specific write protection is implemented. Path: DMI -> dm_csrs -> haltreq -> csr_regfile debug_req_i -> csr_we Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Debugger can modify any machine-mode CSR (including mstatus, mtvec, pmp config) without restriction.

findings.summary: The design lacks any lock-bit mechanism for CSR write protection.
findings.vulnerability_category: write to a locked CSR / locked CSR register / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: any write path (CPU, debug, test) Operation: write to a locked CSR Object: locked CSR register Expected guard: hardware lock bit masking write enable Observed behavior: No lock bits exist in the design. Search for 'lock' in csr_regfile.sv returned no results. Thus, no write protection mechanism is present. Path: csr_we generation -> register write Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Critical CSRs (if any were intended to be locked) can be overwritten at any time.

findings.summary: testmode_i is unbounded and can be used to bypass security features.
findings.vulnerability_category: assert testmode_i / security-critical signals (debug, CSR) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: test controller (testmode_i) Operation: assert testmode_i Object: security-critical signals (debug, CSR) Expected guard: testmode disabled in production or gated by secure lifecycle Observed behavior: testmode_i directly controls clock muxes in the JTAG TAP and is passed to submodules without any gating. No fuse or tie-off is present to disable it. Path: testmode_i -> i_dft_tck_mux in dmi_jtag_tap, and testmode_i inputs of dm_csrs, dmi_jtag, dm_top Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An attacker might use testmode to bypass security checks or extract internal state.

findings.summary: No authentication is required before halting a hart, enabling full system takeover.
findings.vulnerability_category: issue halt request / hart (debug_mode_o) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external debugger via DMI Operation: issue halt request Object: hart (debug_mode_o) Expected guard: authentication before haltreq Observed behavior: haltreq is directly taken from dmcontrol_q.haltreq with no authentication check. Path: DMI write to DMControl.haltreq -> dm_csrs haltreq_o -> dm_top debug_req_o -> csr_regfile debug_req_i Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Unauthenticated debugger can halt any hart and gain full debug control.

findings.summary: ROM2 is writable, contradicting its intended read-only nature.
findings.vulnerability_category: write to ROM2 address space / ROM2 memory array / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: any bus master Operation: write to ROM2 address space Object: ROM2 memory array Expected guard: we_i must always be 0 Observed behavior: rom2 module accepts write when we_i is high. No hardware prevents we_i from being asserted. Path: AXI slave -> axi2mem -> rom2_req/rom2_we -> i_rom2.we_i Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Keys stored in ROM2 can be corrupted or replaced, leading to total security compromise.

findings.summary: No lock bits present in the design; therefore, no secure default exists.
findings.vulnerability_category: reset / any lock-bit register / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: system reset Operation: reset Object: any lock-bit register Expected guard: lock bits must reset to locked state Observed behavior: No lock bits exist in csr_regfile. Search for 'lock' returned nothing. Therefore, no protection can be active. Path: N/A Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Sensitive CSRs cannot be locked, leaving them vulnerable to modification.

findings.summary: Debug SBA can read bootrom, exposing secure boot code.
findings.vulnerability_category: read from bootrom / bootrom instance i_bootrom / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug module SBA Operation: read from bootrom Object: bootrom instance i_bootrom Expected guard: debug SBA requests should be filtered by address range Observed behavior: No address filtering is present. The SBA can set sbaddress to ROM base (0x00010000) and read back data. Path: dm_top -> i_dm_sba -> AXI master -> crossbar -> i_bootrom (slave for ROM) Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Secure boot code and other ROM contents can be extracted by the debugger.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
